title: Aptitude: Trigonometry & Height and Distances - Q1

content.problemStatement:
### **Aptitude Practice Question**

From the top of a lighthouse, the angles of depression of two ships are 30 and 45 degrees. The two ships, as it was observed from the top of the lighthouse, were 100 m apart. Find the height of the lighthouse.<sp

---

### **Answer & Explanation**

an>Here, we can apply the formula Height = Distance/[cot(original angle) – cot(final angle)]

Height of the lighthouse = 100 / (cot 30 – cot 45) 
100 / (√3 – 1) = 50 √3 + 50 m
